metadata.description: Skills marketplace for Claude Code and other AI agents

plugins.name: clipboard
plugins.description: Copy core content of the last answer to the macOS clipboard via the clipboard-copy helper (pbcopy for plain, pbcopy-html for Slack rich text).

plugins.name: bonsai
plugins.description: Worktree lifecycle manager. Create a worktree with branch and put a `cd <worktree> && claude "..."` start command on the clipboard so you can paste it into any terminal pane/tab/app, or prune worktrees with safety checks. Requires macOS.

plugins.name: dont-do-that
plugins.description: Guardrail hooks that push back on common AI reflexes: shifting blame, stopping prematurely, delegating verification, asking for confirmation when none was needed, and em-dashes in prose.

plugins.name: export-skill
plugins.description: Export a skill for sharing. Orchestrator plus five user-invocable sub-skills: sanitize (PII + security), translate (en/nl), port (linux/windows/macos), package (zip or md), share (clipboard + Finder handoff).

plugins.name: eye-of-the-beholder
plugins.description: Visual layout review skill. Observation before explanation: screenshot, describe what you see, then diagnose. Design-TDD for CSS.

plugins.name: ground
plugins.description: Verify Claude's recent output with external sources when the user expresses skepticism. Ad-hoc retrieval-augmented verification.

plugins.name: gurus
plugins.description: Opinionated panels that challenge a decision from multiple perspectives. Three user-invocable skills: gurus:software (eight-persona code review), gurus:council (five-advisor critical thinking with anonymised peer review and chairman synthesis), gurus:gurus (orchestrator that routes by context). All voices share the gurus:sonnet-max subagent.

plugins.name: inspiratie
plugins.description: Online research workflow for unfamiliar topics, design decisions, and evaluating approaches. Forces external perspectives before committing to one path.

plugins.name: recap
plugins.description: Structured status overview of the current session: what we are doing, where we are, what is next. Useful when returning to a session after idle time, after compaction, or after a long stretch of repetitive background output.

plugins.name: recursion
plugins.description: Nightly workflow-improvement loop. /recursion orchestrates schedule, state, focus, and reject. Ships with an internal research sub-skill that runs the deep research workflow producing atomic improvement plans.

plugins.name: rename-suggestion
plugins.description: Generate a descriptive session name based on conversation context and copy the rename command to clipboard.

plugins.name: saysay
plugins.description: Speech mode via macOS say command. Claude speaks output aloud after every response, translating screen content to spoken language.

plugins.name: screen-recording
plugins.description: Automated screen recordings and demo videos of browser-based features using Playwright video recording.

plugins.name: self-improvement
plugins.description: Update CLAUDE.md and skills based on user feedback. Detects duplication, determines optimal location for new instructions, and extracts large sections into standalone skills when CLAUDE.md gets too long.

plugins.name: testing-philosophy
plugins.description: TDD principles, Cucumber/Gherkin conventions, flaky test handling, and test suite health. Red-Green-Refactor workflow.

plugins.name: whywhy
plugins.description: Drill N layers deep into a question or goal (default 7). Claude autonomously asks and answers 'why?' N times, then analyzes the chain for a better direction.

plugins.name: how-plugins-work
plugins.description: Explains how Claude Code plugin naming, skill resolution, and the plugin:skill invocation pattern work in practice. Ships a test-before-push sub-skill that encodes the canonical local-marketplace procedure for testing plugin changes in a fresh session without pushing first.

plugins.name: autonomous
plugins.description: Dispatch a rover at a task. You stay back, the rover works in the field; the distance means it must decide autonomously. A decide framework picks the path, a pride check catches blind spots, and the rover only reports done when the mission is solid. No hard dependencies on personal or team skills.

plugins.name: gitgit
plugins.description: Bundle of git-write skills for Claude Code: commit-all-the-things groups every uncommitted change into logical commits, commit-snipe stages only what belongs to the current task, rebase-latest-default rebases the current branch on the freshest default, and merge-to-default lands a feature branch on the project's default branch with a github-style --no-ff merge commit (rebases on conflict, deletes the local source branch after a confirmed merge, and no-ops with a TUI warning when invoked on the default branch itself).
